note: Election integrity — done responsibly. We explain how voting is secured, how to REPORT a problem or suspected fraud through official channels, and where to read VERIFIED audit results. We do NOT publish unverified 'suspicion' about specific polling places — that would spread misinformation. Flagging a specific site would require an authoritative, verifiable record (a certified audit, court finding, or election-board report).
secured: Most U.S. votes are cast on paper ballots (or produce a paper trail) that can be recounted by hand.
Risk-limiting & post-election audits compare paper ballots against the machine count.
Voting machines are tested before each election (logic & accuracy testing) and are not connected to the internet during voting.
Bipartisan poll workers and observers watch the process; ballots follow a documented chain of custody.
Results are unofficial until canvassed and certified by election officials.
